content.claim: School consolidation produces transportation cost offsets that reduce net fiscal savings: small/sparse districts systematically spend more on student transportation and federal transportation funds per pupil, creating an opportunity cost rarely fully counted in closure proposals.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Hungarian transportation infrastructure and costs may differ from U.S. context
Magnitude of offset highly dependent on local geography
No Hungarian-specific transportation cost data provided in sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
